title: Hypotension and Hyperpigmentation - Addison Disease
chief_complaint: Progressive fatigue, dizziness, weight loss, and darkening of skin over 6 months. Salt craving. Hypotension noted on multiple checks.

physical_exam.general: Hyperpigmentation of palmar creases, buccal mucosa, and scars

hidden_data.acth_stim: No response to ACTH stimulation test

patient_responses.fatigue: I can barely get out of bed
patient_responses.dizziness: I feel lightheaded when I stand up
patient_responses.skin: My skin has been getting darker, especially my scars and gums

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Addison disease - the hyperpigmentation is a classic clue.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is Addison disease. The low cortisol, high ACTH, and hyperpigmentation are classic.
feedback.attending_note: Addison disease: Hydrocortisone 15-25mg/day divided (largest dose AM), Fludrocortisone 0.05-0.2mg/day. Stress dose steroids for illness/surgery. Medical alert bracelet. Well done identifying this rare condition.
